QF.id: VII Market Participants in Securities Markets_41

question.text: Sections 16 and 17 of PMLA deal with:

answer.text: Powers of authorities to carry out summons, searches, and seizures

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Sections 16 and 17 of PMLA lay down the powers of authorities to carry out summons, searches, and seizures during money laundering investigations. Section 6 deals with Adjudicating Authority appointment, Sections 25-26 with Appellate Tribunal, and Section 3 with the definition. **The Trap:** Students often mix up PMLA section numbers. Section 3 = definition, Section 4 = punishment, Section 6 = AA appointment, Section 12 = record keeping, Section 24 = burden of proof. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XXIV covers PMLA structure in detail. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** PMLA key sections: 3 (definition), 4 (punishment), 6 (AA), 9 (confiscation to CG), 11 (AA powers), 12 (records), 12AA (enhanced CDD), 16-17 (search/seizure), 24 (reverse burden), 25-26 (Appellate Tribunal), 43 (Special Courts).
